data.title: Louie DiPalma Reiterates Buy on Booz Allen, Citing Expanded $2.5B AI Defense Contract and Strong Pipeline
data.description:
William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma has maintained their bullish stance on BAH stock, giving a Buy rating on May 26.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Louie DiPalma has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Booz Allen’s expanding role in defense-focused artificial intelligence. He highlights the company’s renewal of its Enterprise Machine Learning Analytics and Persistent Services contract at a higher ceiling value of $2.5 billion over five years, replacing a smaller prior award and signaling strong client confidence in Booz Allen’s capabilities.

DiPalma also points to Booz Allen’s long-standing integration of machine learning and advanced analytics into critical defense and intelligence missions as a competitive strength within the broader AI ecosystem. In addition, he notes that, when factoring in a sizable Army contract that appears likely to be awarded to the firm, Booz Allen is positioned for another year of robust contract wins, supporting the positive outlook that underpins his Buy recommendation.

In another report released on May 26, Stifel Nicolaus also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a $110.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on Karman, Viewing Share Pullback as Technical and Backed by Long-Term Space and Defense Growth
data.description:
William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma has maintained their bullish stance on KRMN stock, giving a Buy rating on May 20.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Louie DiPalma has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including his view that the recent share decline is driven more by technical pressures from the secondary offering and investor concerns around New Glenn than by any deterioration in Karman’s fundamentals. He emphasizes that New Glenn currently accounts for only a modest portion of total revenue, albeit a more meaningful share of the space segment, and had been expected to support robust organic growth through 2025.

He argues that Blue Origin is likely to continue ramping New Glenn production to address its sizable launch backlog, which should sustain demand for Karman’s highly specialized launch components. In addition, Karman’s broader portfolio across space, missile, maritime, and drone markets, combined with forthcoming management commentary at William Blair’s growth conference, underpins his confidence in the company’s long-term growth trajectory despite near-term stock volatility.

In another report released on May 20, KeyBanc also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $100.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Corey Tarlowe Maintains Sell on Dollar Tree, Lifts Price Target to $85 Amid Concerns Over Traffic and Margin Sustainability
data.description:
Analyst Corey Tarlowe from Jefferies maintained a Sell rating on Dollar Tree and increased the price target to $85.00 from $80.00.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Corey Tarlowe has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors tied to the quality and sustainability of Dollar Tree’s recent performance. While the company delivered better-than-expected sales, margins, and earnings, much of the same-store sales growth came from larger basket sizes and higher prices rather than more shoppers, leaving overall traffic still in decline and suggesting that gains may not be durable in a competitive retail landscape.

Corey Tarlowe’s rating is based on concerns that management’s expectation for a traffic rebound in the second half may be optimistic given ongoing pressure on lower-income consumers and the risk that these customers migrate to rivals. Even though he raised his forecasts and increased the price target to $85 to reflect better execution, he remains cautious that heavy dependence on pricing, along with potential margin headwinds from costs such as fuel and tariffs, could limit upside and justify a more conservative stance on the stock.

In another report released today, BMO Capital also maintained a Sell rating on the stock with a $90.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Yeti Wins Buy Rating on Raised Guidance, Strong Demand, and Multiple EPS Upside Levers
data.description:
William Blair analyst Phillip Blee has reiterated their bullish stance on YETI stock, giving a Buy rating on May 15.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Phillip Blee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Yeti’s operating momentum and growth outlook. Following a robust first quarter, management lifted full-year sales and earnings guidance, and Blee sees continued strength in underlying demand with no clear signs of consumer trade-down despite recent pricing actions and a mixed macro environment.

He also highlights the potential for upside beyond management’s current 7%–8% sales growth framework, with a realistic path toward high single- to low double-digit growth supported by wholesale channel expansion, international traction, and product innovation. In addition, Blee points to multiple earnings and cash flow levers—favorable tariff assumptions, prospective tariff refunds, and a larger-than-guided share repurchase program—that could drive double-digit upside to his 2026 EPS forecast, reinforcing his positive stance on the shares.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Jason Ader Reiterates Buy on MongoDB as Strong Results, Raised Guidance and AI Tailwinds Support Durable Growth
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jason Ader has maintained their bullish stance on MDB stock, giving a Buy rating on May 27.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Jason Ader has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to MongoDB’s solid execution and outlook. He highlights that the company delivered revenue, operating margin, and EPS results ahead of expectations, powered largely by record Atlas sales, resilient Enterprise Advanced demand, and strong customer metrics such as robust RPO growth and stable net dollar retention.

Ader also emphasizes that management raised both quarterly and full-year revenue and profit guidance, while still embedding conservatism in Atlas and Enterprise Advanced assumptions, creating room for potential upside. In addition, he points to MongoDB’s sharpening product and go‑to‑market strategy and its positioning to benefit as AI-related workloads shift from testing to large-scale production, supporting durable growth and justifying a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on May 27, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $370.00 price target.
